title: Smoky Saudi Arabian Lamb Kebabs
description: A citrusy and chewy Saudi Arabian dish featuring lamb kebabs marinated in a mixture of rice vinegar, olive oil, and spices, then grilled to perfection. This keto-friendly recipe is perfect for a monsoon evening.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together rice vinegar, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and mix well to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill or grill pan with olive oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the lamb kebabs for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145°F.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the lamb kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Traditionally, Saudi Arabian lamb kebabs are served with a side of rice or flatbread, but for a keto-friendly option, serve with a side of roasted vegetables or a salad.
To add a smoky flavor, you can also add a few wood chips to the grill while cooking the kebabs.
For an extra burst of citrus flavor, serve the kebabs with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice.
